steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content:
Complete the square to find the circle's radius:
$$x^2 - 6x + y^2 - 10y - 30 = 0$$
$$(x-3)^2 - 9 + (y-5)^2 - 25 - 30 = 0$$
$$(x-3)^2 + (y-5)^2 = 64$$
Radius $r = \sqrt{64} = 8$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
A circle inscribed in a square touches all four sides, so the side length equals the diameter:
$$\text{side} = 2r = 16$$
$$\text{perimeter} = 4 \times 16 = 64$$
